title: Off Campus Changed G Flip’s Life, And They’re Eyeing A Season 2 Cameo
text:
Off Campus Changed G Flip’s Life, And They’re Eyeing A Season 2 Cameo

This story was originally publish on R29’s Australia edition. The whole world is raving about Off Campus at the moment, and one of the coolest things about the steamy hockey show blowing up is seeing Australian musicians garner worldwide popularity at the same time. G Flip, whose song Bed On Fire was used during Hannah Wells (Ella Bright) and Garrett Graham’s (Belmont Cameli) sexy montage in Episode 4, has been overwhelmed by the response. Their streams and followers have “skyrocketed”, and it has changed the trajectory of their career. While they’ve been big in Australia for a long time, G Flip spent the last five years trying to break into the US and Europe music scene — and the Off Campus feature has put them firmly on the map.

When I sat down to speak with G Flip about the wild journey since the show was released, they told me about how this bump in popularity could open new doors, how proud Selling Sunset’s Chrishell Stause is of them, and why they said yes to Off Campus in the first place. Oh, and G Flip would do anything to get a cameo role in Off Campus Season 2.

Refinery29: Congrats on your success, G Flip! How does it feel to have your song be used in the most iconic sex scene of Off Campus Season 1?

G Flip: I’m so fortunate, and so lucky that I got the best part of the whole series. The whole build up is them getting together, and they played my whole song. So I’m so fortunate and so lucky, and I thank the Off Campus music team so much for using my song, and like it just worked so well.

R29: It’s pretty spicy, what did you think when you watched that scene?

GF: I was like, oh my lord, I haven’t seen this much heterosexual sex in a long time. But no, it was great. It was hot. I really liked how it intercut with everyday things like making lasagna and stuff. I think my song and the scenes in the sequence did it justice, it was so paired well together.

R29: And the song is actually about your experience with a woman. Was it odd to see it with a heterosexual couple?

GF: Not really, because the song’s about giving into your desire of wanting someone. In my experience it was being queer and I desired someone, and I felt like I shouldn’t be getting with them, or wanting to have these desires, or wanting to sleep with this person, and then giving into it, and that euphoric feeling of finally getting together.

It’s not like really a sexy song, it’s more like euphoric and kind of celebratory, and I feel like while Hannah and Garrett are not a queer couple, it has the same feeling. Two people that desire each other, that feel like they shouldn’t be hooking up with each other, because that wasn’t the plan. So although mine came from a queer experience, it’s kind of that same experience of giving into your desire, setting the bed on fire and just making sweet love.

R29: How has this feature changed your life?

GF: So many people are discovering my music and my other songs. Obviously, the data online has skyrocketed. I’m well known in the Australian music scene, and I’ve been very successful in the Aussie music scene, but for five years I’ve lived in LA to try get my artist project off the ground in the US and Europe and the UK and just other places other than Australia. This has really given me an opportunity to help try attempt to make that happen.

Obviously, when festivals look for support slots, they look at your data, and they look at your numbers, and this has really helped me. It shows that I’m streaming not only in Australia but elsewhere. I’m just hoping something comes of it in the next few months. But even if it doesn’t, I’ve gained more fans globally, which I’m just so stoked about, because I just want more people to hear my music.

R29: Is there anything you hope in particular to achieve off the back of this success?

GF: I would obviously love to play Coachella one day, and I would love to do an international support slot. I’ve never supported a big support slot overseas. I’ve done smaller artists in more club-sized rooms, but never a bigger artist, and I want to play more overseas festivals in Europe and the UK including Glastonbury. I just want to branch out and tick some bigger things off off my list.

R29: How did your song get picked, did Josh Heuston help?

GF: I’m not sure if that’s how it directly happened. I know that Josh did really encourage Aussie music to be part of the show, so that’s why there is a lot of Aussie artists. But at the start of January, I got an email from my publishers saying my song Bed On Fire has gone in for a sync of this new hockey series that is called Off Campus, and that it will be used in a love montage, and it might include Garrett’s naked butt in it. I was like, absolutely, we got some butt cheeks. Let’s go.

R29: Off Campus Season 2 is about to start filming. Would you want to do a cameo?

GF: Oh my god, yes. I will learn how to ice skate. I will do anything. I will do whatever. That would be great. I would love to.

R29: Who do we need to call?

GF: I know! Who do we call? I’m up. I’ll get acting lessons, whatever you want.

R29: Has seeing the music live inside someone else’s story change the way you hear the song yourself?

GF: Now every time I play it live, I’m going to be thinking of Garrett’s abs, I reckon. I can’t wait to play it live. I never thought it was like the loudest song, it wasn’t an encore song. But now I’m like, alright, we gotta change up the set list, this is like now maybe my biggest song, so that’s really, really exciting. Me and my band have always had the most fun performing this song, which is so fun that now it’s going to be a lot of people’s favorite song to hear back.

R29: Do you love how this series has championed Aussie artists?

GF: 100%. We have so much good music and good artists that come out of Australia, so the more it can be championed over here in these big blockbuster and extremely successful American or Canadian TV shows, it helps our careers so much. It really does help our global streaming numbers. In streaming, you can get stuck in an Australian cycle where you’re just paired next to other Aussie artists, so only Aussies really discover you. To break out of that streaming cycle can be really hard.

So, when shows put you in sync, and then you get numbers outside of Australia, it really can help transform your career, and then you have the data to show Coachella, or these other festivals that I do have fans over here, it can help sell tickets.

R29: And how proud is Chrishell?

GF: She’s so proud, it’s really cute. She’s got the iPad set up in her closet, just playing my songs over and over on loop to help me get streams, which is very, very cute. So, she’s super supportive.

This interview has been edited and condensed for clarity.

Stream Off Campus Season 1 on Prime Video now.

title: Don’t Want To Smell Like Anyone Else? Look For This One Perfume Note
text:
Don’t Want To Smell Like Anyone Else? Look For This One Perfume Note

When it comes to buying perfume, I’m the type of person who judges a book by its cover. I can usually tell whether something is for me just by reading the product description. One word that invariably leads me to abandon my cart? Gourmand — scents built around edible, dessert-esque notes like vanilla, caramel, chocolate, or coffee.

Gourmand perfumes have been everywhere for a while now: a recent trend report found that 42% of beauty shoppers have a preference for this fragrance category, driven by our collective appetite for things that feel comforting and nostalgic. Still, I’ve always wrinkled my nose at “sweet treat” gourmand scents, as they can skew too saccharine and juvenile for me. That all changed when I discovered perfumes that feature rice as an ingredient.

Having grown up in China, I find the smell of steamed rice deeply nostalgic, and it helps me feel connected to my culture. It was the aroma that signaled dinner was ready, and everyone in the family was expected to gather around. As the youngest person at the table, it’s my job to serve the rice, and I have fond memories of that humid, welcoming scent wafting into my personal space as soon as I lifted the lid of the cooker.

In perfumery, rice is a component that’s equal parts wearable and highly versatile, depending on what it’s paired with. “Rice brings a soft, comforting quality to a fragrance, but more importantly, it adds texture,” explains Su Min, co-founder of Korean fragrance house Elorea. Rice is a key note in the brand’s Cloud Daze eau de parfum for a reason: “It has a gentle, understated sweetness that feels smooth and almost skin-like rather than sugary.” This, she says, helps blend all the notes harmoniously.

Ahead, find 10 unique rice gourmand perfumes that I never get sick of — and have serious potential as the next IYKYK editor-favorite fragrance.

Glossier You Soie Eau de Parfum

The best way to describe Soie — the newest addition to the ever-expanding Glossier You perfume universe — is “vacation in a bottle”. Depending on your body chemistry, different notes will pull more strongly on your skin: for some people, the creamy, coconutty goodness of rice milk and tiare water (derived from a type of Tahitian gardenia) really jump out, drawing comparisons to the scent of summer sunscreen and the beach. On me, the first blast smelled a lot more marine, thanks to ambrox, a synthetic molecule that smells like sea salt. Once the perfume settles, it has a drier, sappy profile that reminds me of rice paper. The scent takes me back to sailing trips, and for someone who wants to be near a body of water all the time, it’s deeply compelling.

Ormonde Jayne Champaca Parfum

This is the OG rice perfume, created by British perfumer Linda Pilkington in 2002 when she first launched her niche fragrance brand, Ormonde Jayne. Inspired by her trips to the Indian subcontinent, Pilkington sought to recreate the smell of basmati rice and chai in bustling markets, pairing savoury basmati rice — an unusual note at the time — with honeyed champaca flower, woody bamboo, zesty green tea, and spicy pink pepper. I reach for Champaca a lot in the summer: the tea-like quality feels refreshing in the heat, while the toasted rice gives the pretty florals a more muted depth. It’s a sophisticated, office-friendly scent that never gets boring.

D’Annam Mango Sticky Rice Eau de Parfum

D’Annam — a perfume house hailing from Vietnam — is making some of the most interesting fragrances right now, often spotlighting ingredients rooted in Asian heritage, from Vietnamese iced coffee to Chinese oolong tea. Its latest scent, Mango Sticky Rice, pays tribute to Thailand’s popular dessert, opening with juicy whiffs of mango before settling into resinous sticky rice. It stays just on the right side of sweet and syrupy, and, much like chowing down on the actual dessert on the streets of Bangkok, smelling it never fails to bring a smile to my face. If you prefer something more neutral, the brand’s White Rice Eau de Parfum — with orris, tonka bean, and white musk — is about as photorealistic as it gets.

Kilian Moonlight in Heaven Parfum

I’m not normally drawn to coconut-forward scents, but Moonlight in Heaven is the exception. Tuberose — my favorite heady floral note — sits at the heart, bringing a bright femininity, while rice and lactonic notes kick the sexiness up an octave. This is offset by a medley of tropical fruits: juicy mango, sunny coconut, and zingy grapefruit and lemon. I can smell the fruity ingredients almost immediately, but as it dries down, the fragrance becomes less edible, leaving a sensual trail on the neck and wrists. The best way I can describe it is frolicking between the sheets at a five-star, private island resort — and who doesn’t want to smell like that?

Zara Romance D’Iris Parfum

Ever since Zara began collaborating with Jo Malone CBE, the iconic perfumer and founder of Jo Loves, the retailer has become one of my favorite destinations for perfumes that smell far more expensive than they are. Case in point: Romance D’Iris, a delicate floral beauty that reminds me of Versace Crystal Noir Eau de Toilette, but comes in a larger bottle for under $70. The bouquet of iris and heliotrope leaves a velvety, powdery first impression, while the rice top note adds a slightly vegetal soapiness that keeps it from feeling too traditionally feminine. The longevity is also impressive: I sprayed it on a silk scarf and could still smell it two days later. At this price point for a parfum — a concentration that typically contains around 20 to 30% perfume oil — it’s an absolute steal.

BornToStandOut Dirty Rice Eau de Parfum

Smelling this perfume brings up the core memory of running my hands through buckets of dry rice grains while grocery shopping with my grandma as a child. This is a barely-there, unisex number that isn’t cloying in the slightest, and will be a big hit with anyone who loves skin scents. In other words, it’s a must-try for fans of the original Glossier You. The starchy basmati rice comes through immediately, while milk and almond evoke a familiar embrace with a loved one. The peony reads more like fresh skin than an obvious floral, and it fades quickly, leaving behind a soft, musky warmth. Cetalox (an ambery synthetic compound) further amplifies that second-skin effect.

Diptyque Lilyphéa Eau de Parfum

When it comes to rice perfumes, the more obvious choice from Diptyque would be L’Eau Papier Eau de Toilette, a gorgeous scent inspired by ink and paper that includes a rice steam accord. But I recently had the chance to sit down with Natalie Gracia-Cetto, the esteemed creator of Lilyphéa — one of my all-time favorite perfumes and my signature scent — and learned that rice secretly plays a role here, too. According to Cetto, the fragrance contains a newer, high-quality vanillin, a molecule typically associated with vanilla’s sweet warmth, extracted from rice. “The result is a powdery vanilla that is less sweet and almost animalistic and more vaporous; it’s very different from vanilla flavoring we are used to in gourmand,” she says, adding, “It makes for an unusual combination with the green, sappy notes of violet stem.” I think of the crunchy, dewy leaves of water lilies when I smell this, which is precisely what I want to smell like every day.

Dedcool Mochi Milk Eau de Parfum

If you’re a fan of mochi — the chewy Japanese rice cake dessert — this one is for you. Mochi Milk nails that sweet, powdery rice-flour quality you get when you bite into fresh mochi, but it’s not overly literal. Peach nectar gives it an airy fruitiness, while marshmallow, amber, and sweetened rice milk create the dreamy, “sleeping on a cloud” effect promised on the bottle. Sandalwood, jasmine petals, and incense add a little texture and grassiness, reminding me of wet strawberry stems. I always crave something sweet after the first spritz. It also projects surprisingly well: I can still smell it on myself on a packed train, which is exactly the kind of pick-me-up I need on a miserable commute.

Creed Love in White for Summer Eau de Parfum

Many perfumes are described as “refreshing,” but nothing hits quite like this effervescent creation. This is the summer edition of Creed’s Love in White, a bestseller that was reportedly former First Lady Michelle Obama’s favorite fragrance — enough of a reason to put me onto any scent, to be quite honest. The combination of iris, bergamot, and freesia is so crisp and clean, it makes my skin smell like I’ve just stepped out of a shower. Rice water adds a soft “mushiness”, enveloping the fragrance in a cuddly, intimate warmth, while apple and cranberry bring a juicy brightness. To me, this is the olfactory equivalent of sipping a glass of fruit-infused water straight from the fridge on a hot summer afternoon.

Elorea Cloud Daze Eau de Parfum

Elorea is an emerging K-fragrance brand with a knack for turning traditional Korean ingredients into elegant scents. In Cloud Daze, the key note is makgeolli — a rice wine made by fermenting rice, water, and nuruk, a natural starter. This gives the scent a creamy, lightly tangy, almost effervescent quality. That detail makes a big difference. “Rice on its own brings a soft, clean, and comforting sweetness,” says brand founder, Min. “But when you interpret it as rice wine, you introduce that subtle fermented quality, which adds dimension and lift.” This allows the scent to feel alive on the skin, rather than staying purely sweet. Cognac adds even more booziness to the opening, while dalgona (the Korean honeycomb candy you may remember from the pandemic-era coffee craze) brings a grounding finish.

title: Everything We Know About Off Campus Season 2
text:
Everything We Know About Off Campus Season 2

To anyone with a smartphone and an outrageous screen-time average, it’s clear 2026 is the year we collectively obsessed over steamy hockey romance. If, like many of us here at Refinery29, you’ve already smashed through the first season of Prime Video’s viral sensation Off Campus, based on Elle Kennedy’s beloved book series of the same name, we’ve got some good news to satisfy those cravings. Despite only launching on 13 May 2026, Prime Video has already confirmed a second season of the swoon-worthy drama set at Briar University. Fans can rest easy knowing they won’t have to wait long for more romance, drama, and campus antics.

The first season of Off Campus centres on a fake-dating plot between Hannah Wells (Ella Bright), a musician with big dreams, and ice hockey prodigy Garrett Graham (Belmont Cameli), featuring Josh Heuston (Heartbreak High) as magnetic musician, Justin Kohl. Capturing the hearts and minds of YA fans everywhere, here’s everything we know about Off Campus Season 2 so far — plot teasers, returning cast, and when you can expect it on your screens.

When will Off Campus Season 2 be released?

Before Season 1 of Off Campus aired, it was announced that Season 2 had already been greenlit by Prime Video, making eager fans suspect we might be back at Briar University sooner than we think. Speaking to Variety earlier this month, show creator Louisa Levy confirmed that “all eight scripts are written” for the show’s second season.

While we don’t have an exact release date, wrapping up the Season 1 press tour, stars Ella Bright and Belmont Cameli confirmed they’re about to head straight back into production, with some speculating it could be as soon as June 1.

Who will be in Off Campus Season 2?

Fan-favourites Dean Di Laurentis and Allie Hayes will lead Season 2, which makes sense after that cliffhanger.

Much like Netflix’s Bridgerton, each season of Off Campus is set to follow the relationship arc of different characters. While the second book in Kennedy’s series, The Mistake, follows Logan and Grace, the showrunners have already proven they’re not afraid to deviate from the source material. Season 2 will be mostly based on The Score.

Prime Video announced the new leading couple on Instagram, writing: “One couple made a deal. The next one is keeping the score. From Garrett & Hannah to Dean & Allie — the Off Campus love story continues in Season 2.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Off Campus (@offcampusonprime)

Who will be in Off Campus Season 2?

As for the rest of the main cast, Levy confirmed that Josh Heuston would not be returning for Season 2, but that she would “never say never”. However, this season is focused on Mika Abdalla as Allie Hayes and Stephen Kalyn as Dean Di Laurentis.

Other cast members likely to return include Ella Bright, Belmont Cameli, Antonio Cipriano, Jalen Thomas Brooks, Charlie Evans and Khobe Clarke.

For Off Campus Season 2, India Fowler has been cast to play protagonist Grace Ivers, and Philipa Soo will play Scarlett.

Is there a trailer for Off Campus Season 2?

While we don’t have a trailer for Season 2 just yet, fans are anticipating that filming will wrap sometime in September this year. For now, a Season 1 rewatch will have to suffice.

Where can you watch Off Campus Season 2?

Off Campus will be available to stream exclusively on Prime Video. While we wait for Season 2 to drop, all eight episodes from Season 1 are available now.

title: 6 Movies & TV Shows Coming To Netflix This June
text:
6 Movies & TV Shows Coming To Netflix This June

Summer is almost here! Some people may say that this season is not the time to grab a cozy blanket and snuggle up on the couch with your fave TV and movies, but we think it’s the perfect time to escape the heat and get into some escapism. If you’re wondering what to watch this June, Netflix has plenty of interesting options that will satisfy even the most discerning viewer. We’re talking the return of the rom-com, reality TV, and some serious dramas.

Whether you want to be reunited with the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders, accompany Jennifer Lopez or Zoey Deutch in their new rom-coms, or go on a journey with Aang in Avatar The Last Airbender Season 2, Netflix has you covered. Plus, there’s also a new K-Drama to sink your teeth into. Ahead, here are all our top picks for Netflix movies and TV shows to watch this June 2026.

DashDividers_1_500x100

Office Romance

We’re in a rom-com renaissance right now, and it’s glorious. This fun romance follows Daniel (Brett Goldstein) and Jackie (Jennifer Lopez) as they embark on a forbidden romance while working together. Lopez plays a tough CEO of an airline, with her soft side coming out when she finds herself spending more time with Daniel. Written by Goldstein, the comedy looks spicy, fresh and hilarious.

When: June 5
Watch if you like: Going The Distance, 50 First Dates

DashDividers_1_500x100

Teach You A Lesson

This K-Drama series follows a team of unconventional inspectors trying to whip students into shape. There appears to be plenty of action, comedic moments, and lots of drama.

When: June 5
Watch if you like: Girl From Nowhere, Zom 100

DashDividers_1_500x100

America’s Sweethearts: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders Season 3

Join the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders from auditions throughout the entire NFL season. The Emmy-Award winning series follows the team along with senior director Kelli Finglass and head choreographer Judy Trammell.

When: June 16
Watch if you like: Glitter & Gold, Simone Biles Rising

DashDividers_1_500x100

I Will Find You

Sam Worthington joins forces with a stacked cast including Britt Lower, Milo Ventimiglia and Erin Richards in this drama series. Worthington plays an innocent man serving life in prison for the murder of his son. When he finds out his son may still be alive after all, he is determined to break out of prison to save him.

When: June 18
Watch if you like: Trap, Just One Look

DashDividers_1_500x100

Voicemails For Isabelle

Zoey Deutch is the queen of Netflix rom-coms (Set It Up will always be that girl), and Voicemails For Isabelle looks incredible. Deutch plays Jill, who leaves a continuous stream of voicemails for her deceased sister Isabelle, without realising somebody else has her number. Nick Robinson plays Wes, the new owner of Isabelle’s number. When the pair meet in real life, Wes treads the line of deceiving Jill, while also pursuing romance.

When: June 19
Watch if you like: Crazy Stupid Love, Say Anything

DashDividers_1_500x100

Avatar The Last Airbender Season 2

Avatar The Last Airbender returns for a second season, and this time, Aang must master earthbending to end the war. Starring Gordon Comier, Kiawentiio, Ian Ousley, Dallas Liu, Paul Sun-Hyung Lee, Ken Leung and Daniel Dae Kim, this is going to be epic.

When: June 25
Watch if you like: Shadow And Bone, Dragon Ball Z

title: Your June Horoscope Is Here — & You’ll Start The Month With An Exhale
text:
Your June Horoscope Is Here — & You’ll Start The Month With An Exhale

R29 babes, June begins with the kind of exhale many of us have desperately needed. We’re entering the month fresh off the Sagittarius full moon, and collectively, there’s a stronger willingness to release limiting beliefs, old fears, outdated identities, and the mental stories that kept us playing small. The energy feels lighter, more hopeful, and more curious about what life could become instead of obsessing over what already happened. Mercury enters Cancer at the very start of the month, sharpening our intuition and making us more emotionally aware of our environments. Suddenly we feel emotionally safe enough to dream again. And compared to the nonstop astrological plot twists of the first five months of 2026, the majority of June initially feels surprisingly calm.

Gemini Season dominates the first three weeks of the month, and with both the sun and Uranus in Gemini fresh off their conjunction, there’s a collective craving for novelty, movement, experimentation, and possibility. But around June 12th, reality gently taps us on the shoulder. Uranus in Gemini forms a square to the North Node in Pisces, and this may become the first real speed bump of this new Uranian era. It can feel like realizing that inspiration alone is not enough.

Maybe you’ve spent weeks fantasizing about changing your life, moving somewhere new, launching the project, reinventing yourself, escaping the old version of your reality… and then suddenly practical questions emerge. How are you funding this? What systems support it? Are you spiritually aligned with the vision, or just addicted to the fantasy of reinvention? This transit can bring moments where people feel mentally scattered, uncertain which path is actually meant for them. But that discomfort is clarifying. It forces us to separate the visions that genuinely resonate from the ones we were romanticizing because they looked exciting online.

Then the energy starts shifting quickly. Venus enters Leo on June 13th, giving us a preview of the louder, bolder, more expressive energy that will dominate the second half of 2026 once Jupiter enters Leo at the very end of the month. You’ll become more focused on creativity, self-expression, appearance, romance, and visibility. The Gemini New Moon on June 14th encourages us to speak up more honestly about what we want and who we’re becoming.

Then on June 19th, Chiron leaves Aries after more than seven years and enters Taurus, beginning an entirely new collective healing cycle. Chiron in Aries exposed wounds around identity, survival, anger, courage, and individuality. Chiron in Taurus shifts the conversation toward self-worth, money, embodiment, stability, pleasure, and our relationship to the Earth itself. Collectively, we begin learning that healing is not only emotional or spiritual… it’s also physical. It’s learning how to feel safe in our bodies, how to rest without guilt, how to build sustainable lives instead of constantly operating in survival mode.

The solstice arrives on June 21st as the sun enters Cancer and harmonizes beautifully with Chiron in Taurus, softening the atmosphere dramatically. After weeks of mental stimulation and social movement, we’ll start craving intimacy, grounding, and familiarity. And then suddenly, the final days of the month speed everything back up again. Mars enters Gemini on June 28th, making conversations sharper, faster, more reactive, and mentally restless. The very next day, Mercury retrograde begins in Cancer while the Capricorn Full Moon peaks, bringing major themes around emotional maturity, boundaries, responsibility, home life, and long-term goals into focus all at once. The end of June feels like realizing the year is moving much faster than expected.

And finally, on June 30th, Jupiter enters Leo for the first time in over a decade. The tone of 2026 shifts immediately. The second half of the year becomes far more fiery, dramatic, creative, expressive, romantic, passionate, and visible than the softer emotional landscape we experienced with Jupiter in Cancer. You’ll be more willing to take risks, lead publicly, create boldly, and center joy again. June closes by opening an entirely new chapter, one that asks us to stop hiding our gifts and actually let ourselves be seen. And trust me… July will make that impossible to ignore.

Read your horoscopes for your Sun and Rising signs for the most in-depth forecast.

DashDividers_1_500x100

Aries Sun & Rising:

Aries, June 2026 feels like the month where your mind finally catches up to how much your life has already changed. The Gemini stellium dominating the first three weeks of the month lights up your communication, creativity, and local environment sector, and suddenly everything is moving again. This is such a powerful month for writing, marketing, networking, content creation, pitching ideas, traveling short distances, learning new skills, or finally saying the thing you’ve been rehearsing in your head for months. The Gemini New Moon on the 14th especially feels like a reset around your voice and confidence. Some Aries may completely shift how they communicate online or publicly, while others may realize they’ve outgrown old ways of thinking entirely. Your best ideas this month need structure if you actually want them to become reality.

At the same time, there’s a much deeper emotional transition happening underneath everything. Chiron finally leaves your sign on June 19th after more than seven years! You’re not the same person you were in 2018. Chiron in Aries forced you to confront wounds around identity, independence, anger, survival, confidence, and the exhausting pressure to constantly prove yourself. You learned how to fight for yourself, but also how loneliness can hide inside hyper-independence. Now Chiron moves into Taurus and begins healing your relationship with money, security, self-worth, and stability. The focus shifts from “Who am I?” to “What actually makes me feel safe?” And as Cancer Season begins two days later, activating your home and emotional foundation sector, things become softer, more sentimental, more reflective. You may care less about impressing people and more about building a life that genuinely feels nourishing to live in privately.

Then the final days of June arrive and suddenly everything speeds up again. Mars enters Gemini on the 28th, making your thoughts faster and your schedule fuller. At the same time, Mercury retrograde begins in Cancer, bringing old family dynamics, emotional memories, or unresolved conversations back into focus. And somehow, in the middle of all this emotional and mental reshuﬄing, the Capricorn Full Moon lights up your career sector while Jupiter enters Leo on June 30th for the first time in over a decade. Translation: visibility is coming. The domestic and emotional clarity you’ve been building all month is preparing you for a second half of 2026 where people see you much more clearly… and where you finally allow yourself to be seen too.

Taurus Sun & Rising:

Taurus, June 2026 feels like your nervous system slowly realizing the earthquake is over… even if your body still occasionally braces for impact out of habit. The Gemini stellium dominating the first three weeks of the month activates your money, self-worth, and security sector, and because Uranus is now fully in Gemini, you’re entering an entirely different relationship with change. The past seven plus years forced transformation onto you through identity shifts, instability, unexpected endings, reinventions, relocations, breakups, breakthroughs, and versions of yourself dying before you fully had time to grieve them. Now the lesson changes. Uranus in Gemini is teaching you how to adapt mentally and financially instead of personally detonating your entire identity every five business days. You’re learning new ways of making money, new skills, new forms of self-expression, and new definitions of stability altogether. Mars moving through your sign most of the month helps tremendously because it grounds you while all this mental movement happens. You’re still changing, but now there’s more embodiment attached to it. Greater intention and awareness of your pace.

Then June 19th arrives, and Chiron enters your sign for the first time in decades. You’ll feel this transit more personally than almost anyone because it’s happening directly in your identity sector, right after Uranus finally finished reshaping it. And yes, some old wounds may surface around self-worth, body image, security, trust, abandonment, finances, or the fear that your needs are “too much” for others. But what’s different about Chiron in Taurus is that the healing comes through consistency, softness, embodiment, boundaries, rest, nourishment, pleasure, and choosing yourself repeatedly instead of abandoning yourself to maintain comfort for others. Healing during this era may look less dramatic and more practical. Eating better. Leaving environments that drain you. Learning to say no without overexplaining. Finally believing your comfort matters too. And as Cancer Season begins immediately afterward, activating your communication and creativity sector, your softer side becomes much easier to access. You may speak more vulnerably, create more honestly, or reconnect with parts of yourself that became emotionally guarded during Uranus in Taurus survival mode.

Then the final days of June begin rearranging everything again. Mars leaves your sign on the 28th and enters Gemini, shifting your focus toward finances, self-esteem, and how you value your time and energy. Mercury Retrograde begins in Cancer the very next day, bringing old conversations, memories, sibling dynamics, creative ideas, or emotional truths back around for reevaluation. The Capricorn Full Moon at the end of the month feels surprisingly clarifying for you because it activates your expansion sector, helping you recognize how much your worldview has already changed this year. And then Jupiter enters Leo on June 30th, beginning a completely new chapter around home, family, and your private life for the next year. Some Tauruses will move, while others will renovate, grow their families, reconnect with their roots, or finally build the emotional foundation they’ve spent years searching for externally.

Gemini Sun & Rising:

Gemini, June 2026 has you stepping into a version of your life that hasn’t fully existed before. The sun, Mercury, Uranus, and the New Moon all move through your sign during the first half of the month, so the focus is undeniably on you: your identity, your appearance, your voice, your choices, your future, your reinvention. People are noticing you more, but the bigger story is that you’re noticing yourself differently too. Uranus in your sign is still brand new, and there’s this restless excitement in the air where part of you wants to change everything immediately while another part is realizing reinvention is a process, not a random impulse. The Gemini new moon on the 14th feels especially important because it pushes you to speak more honestly about who you are becoming instead of staying loyal to older versions of yourself just because other people are comfortable with them.

Then around June 19th, the tone shifts inward when Chiron enters Taurus and begins activating your solitude, healing, and subconscious sector. After all the external movement of the past few years, this transit may initially feel strange because it asks you to slow down emotionally and spiritually in ways you usually avoid by staying mentally occupied. Chiron in Taurus begins exposing the quiet places where burnout, emotional exhaustion, grief, avoidance, or self-abandonment have been sitting underneath the surface. Chiron will teach you how to rest without guilt and trust your intuition more deeply. And as Cancer Season begins two days later, activating your money and self-worth sector, there’s a growing realization that emotional security and financial security are deeply connected for you now. The more emotionally grounded you become, the easier it becomes to stop settling for situations that undervalue you.

Then the final days of June hit and suddenly everything speeds up again. Mars enters your sign on the 28th, giving you a major burst of confidence, motivation, visibility, sex appeal, and a little extra chaos too because your mind starts moving at lightning speed again. Then Mercury Retrograde begins in Cancer the very next day, forcing you to slow down around money, spending habits, self-worth, and communication around finances. The Capricorn Full Moon brings a major culmination around intimacy, emotional vulnerability, debt, investments, or shared resources. And finally, Jupiter enters Leo on June 30th, beginning one of the most socially expansive and creatively stimulating years you’ve had in over a decade. The second half of 2026 wants you visible, expressive, curious, and fully participating in your own life again.

Cancer Sun & Rising:

Cancer, June 2026 begins quietly, almost like the universe is asking you to listen before you move. The Gemini stellium dominating the first three weeks of the month activates your solitude, healing, and subconscious sector, so while everyone around you seems busy constantly moving, part of you is pulling inward. Your dreams may become more vivid, and your intuition sharpens. This is a deeply restorative month for you mentally and spiritually if you allow yourself enough silence to process what’s actually been happening in your life since the beginning of the year. And with Uranus newly in Gemini, there’s also this strange feeling that your inner world is changing faster than your outer world right now. Some Cancers may suddenly outgrow friendships, identities, routines, or even old ambitions privately before anyone else notices externally. Protect your peace carefully this month because you’re absorbing far more than usual.

Then June 19th arrives, and this is one of the most important astrological shifts you’ve had in years. Chiron leaves Aries after more than seven years of forcing you to confront wounds around ambition, visibility, authority, pressure, and the exhausting feeling that you always had to “hold it together” publicly no matter what was happening emotionally behind the scenes. Now Chiron enters Taurus and begins healing your relationship with friendship, community, collaboration, and belonging itself. You’re entering an era where healing comes through realizing you don’t have to carry life alone. And once Cancer Season begins on June 21st, it brings the energy fully back into your sign. You become more magnetic, emotionally expressive, intuitive, creative, and visible almost immediately.

Then the final days of June arrive and everything suddenly becomes much louder relationally. Mars enters Gemini on the 28th, stirring up your subconscious mind and making rest much harder if you don’t intentionally slow yourself down. Then Mercury Retrograde begins in your sign on June 29th, bringing identity shifts, past relationships, or unresolved feelings back around for review. You may feel temporarily uncertain about where exactly you’re headed next, but that pause is purposeful. The Capricorn Full Moon the same day lights up your relationship sector, bringing major clarity around partnerships, commitments, emotional reciprocity, and whether certain connections still align with who you’re becoming. And finally, Jupiter leaves your sign and enters Leo on June 30th, beginning a year-long focus on money, self-worth, stability, and abundance. The second half of 2026 starts teaching you that emotional fulfillment and material fulfillment aren’t meant to exist separately anymore.

Leo Sun & Rising:

Leo, June 2026 feels like your social life, future vision, and sense of possibility all expanding at the same time. The Gemini stellium dominating the first three weeks of the month activates your friendship, collaboration, and long-term goals sector, so suddenly life feels bigger again. The Gemini New Moon on the 14th is especially powerful for reconnecting with dreams that got buried underneath survival mode these past few years. Some Leos may launch projects with friends, join new communities, meet people who radically shift their perspective, or realize their future looks completely different from what they imagined even six months ago. Uranus in Gemini is pushing you toward people and spaces that feel mentally stimulating and future-oriented, but discernment matters. Not every exciting connection is meant to become deeply intimate. Some people are simply catalysts helping you remember how much life still has left to offer you.

Then June 19th changes the tone completely when Chiron enters Taurus and begins activating your career and reputation sector for the next several years. Chiron in Aries spent the past seven plus years forcing you to confront wounds around belief systems, freedom, identity expansion, travel, education, and trusting your own path even when others didn’t understand it. Now the healing becomes more visible and public. Questions around success, recognition, leadership, and being fully seen begin surfacing in a much deeper way. Some Leos may realize they’ve spent years chasing external validation while quietly disconnecting from themselves in the process. Others may finally allow themselves to pursue work that actually feels emotionally meaningful instead of simply impressive. And once Cancer Season begins on June 21st, your emotional world becomes much more private and introspective. You may suddenly crave more rest, solitude, therapy, spiritual grounding, or emotional retreat before the next major chapter fully begins.

And then the end of June arrives like a movie trailer for the second half of 2026. Mars enters Gemini on the 28th, rapidly energizing your social sector and making life busier, louder, and far more collaborative. Mercury Retrograde begins in Cancer immediately afterward, bringing hidden emotions, subconscious patterns, burnout, or unresolved grief to the surface if you’ve been avoiding rest. The Capricorn Full Moon on June 29th highlights your health, routines, and daily life, pushing you to get honest about whether your lifestyle can actually sustain the future you say you want. And then the big one arrives: Jupiter enters your sign on June 30th for the first time in over a decade. This is huge for you. The second half of 2026 wants you leading, creating, expressing yourself boldly, and finally taking up the space you’ve been slowly growing into all along.

Virgo Sun & Rising:

Virgo, June 2026 has you standing at the edge of a completely different future and realizing you’re actually ready for it now. The Gemini stellium dominating the first three weeks of the month activates your career and visibility sector, so your public life speeds up quickly. Some Virgos may receive unexpected opportunities, shift career directions entirely, gain more visibility online, or suddenly feel called to pursue goals that would’ve intimidated them a year ago. The Gemini New Moon on the 14th especially feels like a reset around your professional identity. You’re realizing you can evolve publicly without having every detail figured out first. Uranus in Gemini is teaching you how to adapt instead of over-controlling every outcome, which is not always your favorite lesson. Your challenge this month is avoiding mental burnout from trying to optimize every possibility simultaneously. Prioritize what actually matters instead of treating your life like an endless self-improvement project.

Then June 19th arrives, and this shift may feel surprisingly relieving. Chiron leaves Aries after more than seven years of exposing wounds around vulnerability, trust, intimacy, and the fear of relying too heavily on others. Now Chiron enters fellow Earth sign Taurus and begins healing your relationship with expansion, belief, purpose, travel, spirituality, and possibility itself. You’re entering an era where healing comes through allowing life to become larger again instead of emotionally shrinking yourself to stay safe. Some Virgos may travel more, study something deeply meaningful, reconnect spiritually, teach, or completely change the philosophy guiding their life. And once Cancer Season begins on June 21st, your friendships and community life soften too. You may feel more emotionally connected to the people around you and more aware of which relationships genuinely support the person you’re becoming versus the version of you that existed purely in survival mode.

Then the final days of June shift the energy dramatically. Mars enters Gemini on the 28th, intensifying your ambition, visibility, and workload for the next six weeks. Things move fast professionally, and people may expect more from you almost overnight. Then Mercury Retrograde begins in Cancer, bringing friendship dynamics, old collaborations, past goals, or unresolved social situations back around for reevaluation. The Capricorn Full Moon on June 29th lights up your creativity, romance, and pleasure realm, helping you reconnect with what actually makes life enjoyable instead of purely productive. And finally, Jupiter enters Leo on June 30th, beginning a much quieter but deeply spiritual chapter for you. The second half of 2026 becomes less about external hustle and more about inner alignment, healing, intuition, and preparing emotionally for the massive personal rebirth that arrives once Jupiter eventually enters your sign next year.

Libra Sun & Rising:

Libra, June 2026 feels like your world getting bigger mentally, spiritually, creatively, and maybe geographically too. The Gemini stellium dominating the first three weeks of the month activates your expansion sector, so there’s a strong craving for movement and perspective shifts. Some Libras may suddenly want to travel, study something new, relocate, publish work, teach, date outside their usual “type,” or simply stop limiting themselves to the same routines and environments that have been emotionally suffocating lately. The Gemini New Noon on the 14th feels especially liberating because it reminds you that your life does not have to stay confined to old expectations, including the expectations you quietly placed on yourself. Uranus in Gemini is helping you detach from rigid ideas of who you’re “supposed” to become. Conversations can completely alter your direction this month, so stay open, but discerning.

Then June 19th shifts the atmosphere much deeper when Chiron enters Taurus and activates your intimacy, vulnerability, and emotional merging zone. Chiron in Aries spent the past seven plus years exposing wounds around relationships, people-pleasing, abandonment fears, codependency, and the exhausting pressure to keep partnerships balanced even when you were quietly disappearing inside them. Now Chiron in Taurus begins teaching you how to feel safe in intimacy without abandoning your own needs in the process. Money, trust, sexuality, emotional vulnerability, shared resources, and power dynamics all become part of the healing conversation now. And once Cancer Season begins on June 21st, your career sector lights up emotionally too. You may care much more deeply about creating work that actually reflects your heart instead of simply maintaining appearances professionally.

Then Mars enters Gemini on the 28th, bringing momentum, travel, opportunities, learning, and expansion into overdrive for the next six weeks. However, Mercury retrograde begins in Cancer, slowing things down professionally and forcing you to reevaluate career goals, leadership dynamics, or the version of success you’ve been chasing. The Capricorn Full Moon on June 29th highlights your home and emotional foundation sector, bringing important revelations around family, boundaries, or emotional stability. And finally, Jupiter enters Leo on June 30th, beginning one of your most socially abundant and creatively fulfilling years in over a decade. The second half of 2026 wants you visible, connected, collaborative, and surrounded by people who genuinely celebrate your growth instead of quietly competing with it.

Scorpio Sun & Rising:

Scorpio, June 2026 feels like an emotional deep dive followed by a complete shift in direction by the time the month ends. The Gemini stellium dominating the first three weeks of June activates your intimacy, transformation, and merging sector. You’re thinking deeply about trust, money, reciprocity, vulnerability, power dynamics, and who or what you’re genuinely investing your energy into. The Gemini New Moon on the 14th can bring major conversations around finances, debt, contracts, emotional boundaries, sexuality, or relationship dynamics that have quietly been evolving for months. Uranus in Gemini is pushing you to detach from old attachment patterns and rethink what security actually means to you. Some Scorpios may completely change how they merge lives with others emotionally or financially this month. Others may realize they’ve been carrying emotional weight that was never fully theirs to begin with.

Then June 19th arrives and changes the relationship landscape for years to come. Chiron leaves Aries after more than seven years of exposing wounds around burnout, productivity, physical health, and constantly trying to hold everything together through sheer survival instinct. Now Chiron enters Taurus and activates your relationship sector directly. Healing over the next several years will happen through partnership, vulnerability, honesty, boundaries, emotional safety, and learning how to stay connected without losing yourself in the process. Some Scorpios may attract relationships that mirror unresolved wounds around worthiness and trust very quickly. Others may finally understand how hyper-independence has protected them emotionally while also isolating them. And once Cancer Season begins on June 21st, your perspective widens dramatically and you start craving expansion again.

Then the final days of June hit and Mars enters Gemini on the 28th, energizing your transformation sector and making emotions, desires, ambitions, and even financial matters move much faster for the next six weeks. Mercury Retrograde begins in Cancer the next day, bringing old beliefs, travel plans, spiritual questions, or unresolved conversations back into focus. The Capricorn Full Moon on June 29th activates your communication sector, and something you’ve been holding back likely needs to be said by then. And finally, Jupiter enters Leo on June 30th for the first time in over a decade, launching a huge new chapter around career, visibility, ambition, leadership, and public recognition. The second half of 2026 pushes you out of hiding professionally. People are about to see much more of what you’re capable of.

Sagittarius Sun & Rising:

Sag, June starts with other people demanding your attention more loudly than your own inner monologue. The Gemini stellium dominating the first three weeks of the month activates your relationship sector, and suddenly every connection feels like it has something important to teach you. Lovers, exes, collaborators, close friends, rivals, random people you meet once who somehow alter your perspective for weeks afterward… all relationships become mirrors this month. The Gemini New Moon on the 14th can bring surprising beginnings in love, reconciliations, breakups, contracts, or simply a realization that the way you approach partnership has fundamentally changed. Uranus in Gemini adds unpredictability to all of this. You may crave freedom and closeness simultaneously, which can confuse both you and other people if you’re not communicating clearly. Some Archers will realize they’ve outgrown relationship dynamics built around emotional avoidance disguised as “independence.”

Then June 19th shifts your focus toward your body and routines when Chiron enters Taurus. Chiron in Aries spent the past seven plus years exposing wounds around creativity, self-expression, romance, joy, validation, and the fear of fully letting yourself be seen emotionally. Now Chiron in Taurus begins asking whether your daily life actually supports your mental, emotional, and physical well-being. Sleep, stress, diet, work-life balance, nervous system regulation, burnout… none of this can be ignored anymore. Cancer Season beginning on June 21st deepens everything emotionally too. Intimacy, trust, vulnerability, and emotional reciprocity become less abstract concepts and more immediate realities you actually have to sit with instead of philosophizing your way around.

Then June ends by throwing gasoline on the fire. Mars enters fellow Mutable sign Gemini on the 28th, energizing your relationship sector and making attraction, conflict, chemistry, and passion move at lightning speed. Mercury Retrograde begins in Cancer immediately afterward, reopening emotional conversations around trust, money, intimacy, and unresolved emotional entanglements. The Capricorn Full Moon on June 29th brings clarity around finances and self-worth, especially if you’ve been undervaluing your time or energy lately. And finally, Jupiter enters fellow Fire sign Leo on June 30th, beginning one of the most expansive and exciting chapters you’ve had in over a decade. The second half of 2026 wants you bigger: bigger dreams, risks, visibility, and adventures. Your world’s about to expand rapidly, but first June asks whether the relationships in your life can actually grow with you.

Capricorn Sun & Rising:

Capricorn, June 2026 feels like your life quietly reorganizing itself from the inside out, even if outwardly everything still looks relatively stable. The Gemini stellium dominating the first three weeks of the month activates your routines, health, work, and daily systems sector, so the details matter more than usual. How you spend your mornings, how much sleep you’re getting, the way you manage your schedule, your stress, your body, your energy… all of it becomes impossible to separate from your larger goals now. The Gemini New Moon on the 14th is especially useful for resetting habits and creating systems that actually fit the life you’re trying to build instead of the version of yourself that existed three burnout cycles ago. Uranus in Gemini is also shaking up old work patterns. Some Capricorns may completely change their workflow, explore more flexible career structures, work remotely, or realize they no longer want success at the cost of their nervous system.

Then June 19th brings a surprisingly emotional shift when Chiron enters fellow Earth sign Taurus and activates your creativity, romance, and inner child sector. Chiron in Aries spent the past seven plus years exposing wounds connected to home, family, emotional safety, belonging, and the pressure to always be the responsible one. Now the healing becomes softer, more expressive, and more personal. Chiron in Taurus begins teaching you how to enjoy your life again instead of only managing it efficiently. Some Capricorns may reconnect with creativity they abandoned years ago. Others may realize they’ve been emotionally guarded in romance for so long that pleasure itself started feeling unfamiliar. And once Cancer Season begins on June 21st, relationships become emotionally louder too. You may suddenly crave intimacy, partnership, affection, and emotional reciprocity more openly than usual.

Then June closes with a huge energetic shift. Mars enters Gemini on the 28th and accelerates your workload, schedule, and mental pace significantly for the next six weeks. Mercury Retrograde begins in Cancer immediately afterward, bringing relationship reassessments, unresolved emotional dynamics, or former lovers back into focus. The Capricorn Full Moon on June 29th lands directly in your sign, making this one of the most personally significant moments of the month for you emotionally and physically. You’re seeing yourself differently now. And finally, Jupiter enters Leo on the 30th, beginning a year-long chapter focused on intimacy, vulnerability, and shared resources. The second half of 2026 asks you to stop carrying life entirely alone and allow deeper emotional and material support into your world.

Aquarius Sun & Rising:

Aquarius, June 2026 feels like your creativity waking back up after a long period of emotional and mental survival mode. The Gemini stellium dominating the first three weeks of the month activates your romance, pleasure, and self-expression sector, and life starts feeling interesting again. Flirting becomes easier, inspiration returns, and you may suddenly want to create more, post more, travel more, and experiment more. The Gemini New Moon on the 14th feels especially refreshing because it reconnects you with the version of yourself that existed before everything became so serious and heavy these past few years. Uranus in Gemini is also making you much more willing to take creative risks. Some Aquarians may completely reinvent their aesthetic, artistic style, dating life, or public persona this month. Others may realize they’ve spent too much time intellectualizing their feelings instead of actually experiencing them in real time.

Then June 19th shifts the atmosphere in a much more personal direction when Chiron enters Taurus and activates your home and emotional foundation zone. Chiron in Aries spent the past seven plus years exposing wounds around being misunderstood, and the pressure to always explain yourself correctly. Now Chiron in Taurus begins teaching you how to feel safe within yourself emotionally instead of always escaping into the future mentally. Family patterns, childhood memories, housing situations, emotional security, and your relationship to rest itself all become part of the healing process now. And once Cancer Season begins on June 21st, your body starts reacting much faster to stress, overstimulation, and emotional imbalance, so slowing down becomes necessary, not optional.

Then June ends by dramatically accelerating relationship themes. Mars enters Gemini on the 28th, heating up romance, creativity, confidence, and your desire to be seen for the next six weeks. Mercury Retrograde begins in Cancer immediately afterward, forcing you to re-evaluate routines, work dynamics, and boundaries. The Capricorn Full Moon on June 29th activates your solitude and subconscious sector, bringing emotional truths to the surface that you may have been avoiding through distraction or overstimulation. And finally, Jupiter enters Leo on June 30th, beginning one of the most relationship-centered years you’ve experienced in over a decade. The second half of 2026 brings major growth through partnership, collaboration, love, and learning how to stay emotionally present without needing constant escape routes.

Pisces Sun & Rising:

Pisces, June 2026 feels deeply personal from the very beginning… like your emotional world is trying to reorganize itself around who you’re becoming now instead of who you had to be to survive the past few years. The Gemini stellium dominating the first three weeks of the month activates your home, roots, and family sector, so your attention naturally turns inward. Some Pisces may move, redecorate, reconnect with family, spend more time at home, or suddenly realize their nervous system needs a completely different environment to thrive. The Gemini New Moon on the 14th especially feels like a reset around emotional safety. Uranus in Gemini is shaking up old definitions of stability and helping you understand that “home” is not always a place you inherit… sometimes it’s something you consciously create.

Then June 19th shifts the energy in a way you’ll likely feel immediately because Chiron enters Taurus and activates your communication zone. Chiron in Aries spent the past seven plus years exposing wounds around money, survival, self-worth, scarcity, and the fear of not having enough emotionally or materially. Now Chiron in Taurus begins teaching you how to communicate your needs clearly, trust your perspective, and stop shrinking your thoughts to avoid making other people uncomfortable. Some Pisces may begin writing more, teaching, creating content, speaking publicly, or finally saying things they previously swallowed emotionally. And once Cancer Season begins on June 21st, creativity, romance, dating, and inner child healing all become much more available again.

June closes with Mars entering Gemini on the 28th, energizing your home sector for the next six weeks and potentially bringing fast-moving changes around living situations or emotional boundaries. Mercury Retrograde begins in fellow Water sign Cancer immediately afterward, bringing old lovers, creative projects, or unresolved feelings back into focus. The Capricorn Full Moon on June 29th lights up your friendship sector, helping you clearly see which people and environments still align with the future you’re building. And finally, Jupiter enters Leo on June 30th, beginning a year-long chapter focused on health, routines, productivity, wellness, and rebuilding your daily life in a way that genuinely supports your emotional and physical well-being. The second half of 2026 asks you to romanticize your actual life, not just your dreams.

title: The Blue Moon In Sagittarius Is Here — & It’s The Shake Up You Need
text:
The Blue Moon In Sagittarius Is Here — & It’s The Shake Up You Need

The long-awaited Blue Moon in Sagittarius is finally here! Its fiery energy is going to shake life up. May 31st brings the first monthly Blue Moon since August 31st, 2023, and the last one until December 31st, 2028 (a total lunar eclipse). There will be a few seasonal Blue Moons before 2028, but none like this current lunation — it’s a big splash to mark the last days of spring.

This rare lunation is what astronomers and stargazers call a “calendar-month Blue Moon” because it is the second Full Moon in May. The first one occurred on May 1st, known as the Full Flower Moon in Scorpio. Lucky for us, we have a second chance to manifest our desires, let the moon’s illumination bring us clarity, and connect to the universe — or, in this case, shoot from the hip, as well as speak passionately about our worldly aspirations and hopes. Even though it is a micromoon, meaning its apogee is farther from earth than usual, we’ll still feel the effects of the plenilune [a completely full moon]. The moon will still light up the sky and offer us guidance in our personal journeys.

The resourceful and foundational planet Saturn, which is acting passionately in Aries, and transformative Pluto, are motivating us to progress in Aquarius, and gently connect with the Blue Moon. These celestial bodies amplify intuition and offer resilience, confidence, and determination. Visionary Neptune in Aries is in close range, making us feel lofty and idealistic about our beliefs. Basically, it’ll be hard for us to get off our high horses and the need to sway others to our views if they clash with ours. No one will back down from a debate, which can lead to intense arguments that might inflame delicate relationships if we’re not careful, deliberate, or respectful. The warning is not to take matters to the point of no return. 

The asteroids Pallas in Aries and Juno in Aquarius add flavor to the Blue Moon. Pallas is the warrior archetype and extra sparky in Aries. Our impulses urge us to act without thinking or knowing the implications. Juno is loyal until betrayed. In the sign of Aquarius, we can experience it as unconventionality and freedom in partnerships. The flip side is that we may detach from the drama to maintain our peace of being. The two asteroids push us to prioritize our autonomy and value our choices. Rather than listen to everyone’s opinions, honing in on our voice and mindset is vital. 

Watch out: Mercury is in the last degrees of Gemini. This creates a frenetic, immediate need to speak before we put our musings together. Yes, we may put our foot in our mouths and say inappropriate things that could ruffle many feathers — a sentiment the moon in Sagittarius echoes. It’s important to keep track of our ideas and concepts; jumping from A to B can happen in seconds within a conversation or thought process, making it hard to focus on a single point. 

While two beliefs can be true at the same time, we should be aware of our behavior (especially before we generate conflict) to ensure we maintain our composure when the heat rises due to the minor aspect shared with Mercury and Pluto. The following day, Mercury enters Cancer, bringing calm, serene feelings. 

The silver lining is that Mercury harmonizes with the centaur Chiron in Aries, helping us mend fences and heal wounds that surface. There is a fine line between being oppressive and kind. If we listen closely, we’ll hear that everyone is on the same side, just speaking over each other. Therefore, we must be cognizant and conscious of how we come across. 

Fighting for the sake of being controversial and relevant is going to make things messier. The Blue Moon is a curator of chaos, but we don’t have to let it. We can willfully switch to the vibe by agreeing not to agree, nobly walking away from disputes, building bridges toward resolution, or simply allowing others to express their sentiments and acknowledging them with an open mind and heart.

title: I Took NMN Every Day For 30 Days And A Stranger Complimented My Neck
text:
I Took NMN Every Day For 30 Days And A Stranger Complimented My Neck
If you’re like us, you’ve talked yourself out of a lot of good decisions, and into a few bad ones, because you couldn’t tell what was actually worth it. VETTED is here to help. Whether it’s a product, a procedure, or a place, we do the digging, ask the real questions, and try it ourselves before we tell you a thing. If it made the cut, it’s because it earned it.

I have a complicated history with supplements. Most of them are, in my experience, elaborate placebos in attractive packaging. And don’t even get me started on gummy vitamins. So when I kept hearing NMN come up in the longevity space — from The Skinny Confidential crowd to Bryan Johnson’s entire public existence — I did what any reasonable skeptic does. I filed it away and kept scrolling. 

Then I actually listened to David Sinclair on Diary of a CEO, and the science was too loud to ignore. 

I wasn’t looking for a 30-day transformation. I’m in my thirties, I feel good, and I take my health seriously enough to think about who I want to be at ninety, not just next summer. NMN appealed to me as a long game: a cellular investment, the kind of thing you start before you need it. I bought my first bottle at Erewhon more on that tax later and committed to a full 30 days before forming any opinion. 

I did not expect what came next, especially this fast. 

DashDividers_1_500x100

The Product

Novos Boost (NMN) is a warning: really big word incoming nicotinamide mononucleotide supplement built on one core premise: that aging is not inevitable, it’s biological (and biology can be influenced). NMN works by replenishing NAD+, a coenzyme that powers cellular energy production and declines with age. Less NAD+ means less cellular repair, slower metabolism, duller skin, and that creeping fatigue that starts feeling normal in your thirties. 

I’ll be honest. I’d heard the term third-party tested before and filed it away without fully understanding why it mattered. It wasn’t until I heard Dr. Rhonda Patrick break it down on Diary of a CEO that it actually clicked. Here’s the thing that blew my mind: Supplement brands can legally put almost anything on a label, including ingredients that might not actually be inside the supplement. Dr. Patrick used creatine gummies as an example. Most of them contain little to no creatine. Which is exactly why I wasn’t just going to hand over $44 to just any brand.

Third-party testing is my holy grail metric because an external company that has no skin in the game, independently verifies what’s actually in the supplement. I scanned a few options and bought the Novos brand because of the unbiased stamp of approval. Not one, but two accredited third-party labs (Anresco and Micro Quality) confirmed a 100%+ purity and no mercury detected. 

DashDividers_1_500x100

How I Used It

I took two capsules daily with my first meal, which for me lands around noon. I stacked it with a basic women’s multivitamin from Whole Foods nothing elaborate, nothing that would complicate the picture. I completed a full 30 consecutive days, which the brand recommends as the minimum window before drawing any conclusions. On the two days I forgot and took it before bed instead of with food, I still fell asleep without any issue. No racing thoughts, no disrupted sleep, no adjustment period to speak of. The protocol is low-lift, which matters when you’re committing to something long-term.

DashDividers_1_500x100

What I noticed

I want to be clear that I didn’t start taking NMN for a 30-day transformation. I started taking it because I follow the longevity space closely — Sinclair’s explanation was the nudge that finally got me to pull the trigger and I wanted to be building toward the version of myself that’s still sharp, energetic, and vibrant at ninety. I was not expecting to feel anything significant in the first month. That’s not how cellular health works, and I knew that going in. 

Which is exactly why what happened next surprised me. 

The napping stopped. I’m a dedicated weekend napper, the kind who schedules around it. Somewhere in the first two weeks, I just… didn’t need it anymore. The afternoon wall that I’d accepted as part of my life quietly disappeared. The energy shift wasn’t caffeinated or jittery. There was no sudden jolt, no artificial alertness. I just stopped running out of fuel before the day was over. 

Then came the skin comments. I was traveling, which meant my skincare routine had been stripped to the bare minimum, a travel-size PCA Facial Wash Gentle Cleanser and a Youth To The People serum. No elaborate multi-step routine, no new products, nothing to credit. A woman at my nephew’s lacrosse game stopped me to ask what I was doing for my skin. When I told her it was just makeup, she pointed to my neck and said there was no makeup there, and it was glowing anyway. I went home and looked in the mirror more carefully than I had in months. The dullness I’d gotten used to was gone. Something underneath looked different — brighter, more alive. 

Another noticeable difference: my nails. I haven’t seen NMN marketed for nail growth, and I hadn’t heard anyone in the longevity space mention it as a benefit, but somehow in these 30 days, my natural nails got undeniably longer than ever. No polish, no gel, no change to my routine. Just quietly, consistently growing. I can’t say with certainty it was the NMN, but I can say it was the only variable. See for yourself. 

DashDividers_1_500x100

The Price

At $44 for a 30-day supply, Novos Boost NMN sits in the mid-range of the supplement market, more than your average vitamin, less than most of the serums in your bathroom cabinet. I’ll be honest: I bought my first bottle at Erewhon, alongside a vegan pizza and a Hailey Bieber skin glow smoothie. Standing at that register watching the total climb was, truly, a hard pill to swallow — no pun intended. But that’s the thing about Erewhon. It has a way of making you confront exactly how much you’re willing to spend on your wellness era, and apparently my answer is: quite a bit.

My honest wish is that Novos would offer a 60-day bottle at a better per-pill price point, because this is not a one-month experiment. This is a long-term investment in your biology, and the math adds up to over $500 a year. That said, I’ve spent more than $44 on moisturizers that did considerably less. When you reframe it as cellular infrastructure rather than a wellness add-on, the price starts to make sense. You’re either paying now or paying later, the longevity community has been saying this for years, and at this point, the evidence is clear.

DashDividers_1_500x100

Would I Buy It Myself

I’m already on bottle two. I ran out while traveling and can’t wait to get home and back on my routine. That’s not something I’ve ever said about a supplement before — historically I’ve been skeptical of the entire category, gummy vitamins included. The fact that I’m actively anticipating my dose tells you everything about where I landed. 

DashDividers_1_500x100

Who It’s For

I’m not a doctor and I’m not going to pretend to be one (please talk to yours before adding anything new to your routine, especially if you’re managing medications or existing health conditions). What I can tell you is who this feels right for based on my own experience. If you’re like me, someone who has been loyal to her basic women’s multivitamin for years and is starting to wonder if it’s time to level up, this is the natural next step in your big girl glow-up supplement routine. It’s for the woman who is already doing the work and wants to start investing at the cellular level. Not for a quick fix. For the long game. 

If you’re looking for something you’ll feel dramatically by day three, this isn’t your supplement. NMN works on a timeline measured in months and years, not days. NMN interacts with cellular pathways, so it’s always worth making sure your full regimen is working together, not against itself. 

DashDividers_1_500x100

The Verdict

Worth it. When I first opened the bottle and saw how small the capsules were, my immediate reaction was that I had just wasted $44 on very expensive nothing. They are comically, almost insultingly tiny. I stood there holding the bottle thinking about every supplement I’d ever bought that did exactly nothing and wondering if I’d done it again. I hadn’t. But I guess that old cliché about good things coming in small packages actually holds some weight. Thirty days later, I have more energy, visibly better skin, and a second bottle already ordered. My only real critique is the price-to-supply ratio — at $44 for 30 days, the annual commitment is real, and a larger bottle at a lower per-dose price would make suggesting this to everyone significantly easier. But I’m recommending it anyway, price and all. Some investments you make for who you’re going to be in sixty years. This is one of them. 

Skip it if you want fast, visible results. NMN works on a timeline measured in months and years, not days. If that’s not your timeline, this isn’t your supplement. 

About the writer.

Alexis Bennett Parker is the Director of Shopping Partnerships at Refinery29. Strategic consumption is her Olympic sport. And her nine to five.
